message:
feat(console): wire alt-screen split layout into server + fix clippy (br-1m6a.20)

Add LogPane to StartupDashboard, route log lines through ring buffer in
split mode, render two-pane HUD+LogViewer layout in AltScreen, and add
keyboard bindings for scroll/search/follow. Fix clippy warnings from
br-1m6a.7 animated table effects (cast_precision_loss, doc_markdown,
missing_const_fn, too_many_lines). Fix test assertions for box-drawing
panel format change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

STATUS COLORS (using theme constants from br-1m6a.12):
- 2xx: SUCCESS color, bold
- 3xx: INFO color
- 4xx: WARNING color, bold
- 5xx: DANGER color, bold

METHOD COLORS:
- GET: INFO color
- POST: PRIMARY color
- PUT: ACCENT color
- DELETE: DANGER color

ADDITIONAL ELEMENTS:
- Duration: WARNING color (yellow)
- Path: TEXT color, bold
- Client IP: MUTED color

IMPLEMENTATION:
- fn status_style(code: u16) -> Style - returns appropriate style
- fn method_style(method: &str) -> Style - returns appropriate style
- Apply in render_http_request_panel() for request log panels
- Apply in render_dashboard_frame() for the last-request footer

TESTING: Test status_style for 200, 301, 404, 500.  {"id":"br-1m6a.12","title":"Centralized theme system with adaptive colors","description":"Create a centralized semantic theme/palette for *all* console output (banner, tool panels, request panels, HUD), using FrankenTUI's built-in theme system as the single source of truth.

WHY
- We want the console to feel cohesive, branded, and polished (not a pile of ad-hoc ANSI constants).
- FrankenTUI already ships a strong theme system (multiple curated palettes + cached semantic styles + syntax theme integration). We should leverage it rather than reinventing a parallel palette.

CURRENT STATE
- Rich console output is split across:
  - `crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/console.rs` (banner + tool panels + sparkline + toast/log panel helpers)
  - `crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/lib.rs` (HTTP request panel + HUD rendering)
- `console.rs` still uses hardcoded raw ANSI escape code strings (CYAN_B, BLUE, MAG, etc.).

SOURCE OF TRUTH (DO NOT REINVENT)
- Use `ftui_extras::theme`:
  - `ThemeId`, `set_theme`, `current_theme_name`, `current_palette`
  - `semantic_styles_cached()` for consistent `Style` tokens
  - `syntax_theme()` (feature `syntax`) so JSON/tool panels match the active palette

CONFIG (ENABLED BY DEFAULT IN REAL TTYS)
- Add env var `CONSOLE_THEME` (string):
  - Values: `cyberpunk_aurora|darcula|lumen_light|nordic_frost|high_contrast`
  - Default: `cyberpunk_aurora`
- This is console-only (must not affect server semantics).
- Persist `CONSOLE_THEME` alongside other `CONSOLE_*` keys in the user envfile from `br-1m6a.19` so interactive tuning is remembered.

INTEGRATION
1) Startup wiring
- When rich console is active (`LOG_RICH_ENABLED=true` + TTY), set the theme early (before banner/HUD render) by calling `ftui_extras::theme::set_theme(...)`.

2) Replace ad-hoc styling
- Replace hardcoded ANSI constants and scattered color decisions with semantic styles:
  - success/warn/error/info/muted/title/header/link
- When rendering via `Frame` + exporter, rely on `Style` tokens rather than emitting raw SGR.

3) Syntax highlighting alignment
- Any use of `ftui_extras::syntax::SyntaxHighlighter` should be constructed with the active `syntax_theme()` so JSON looks coherent across banner/tool panels.

NON-TTY / RICH DISABLED
- If `stdout.is_terminal()==false` or `LOG_RICH_ENABLED=false`: do not emit rich panels; keep output plain and grep-friendly.

TESTING
- Unit tests for parsing `CONSOLE_THEME` -> `ThemeId` mapping.
- Render/unit tests must use `ftui_extras::theme::ScopedThemeLock` to avoid cross-test theme races.
- Regression test: after migration, no raw `\x1b[` literals remain in `crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/console.rs` except inside exporter/normalizer helpers.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RubyPrairie","created_at":"2026-02-06T20:52:40.649220290Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T23:38:10.015301954Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T23:38:10.015215022Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1m6a.12","depends_on_id":"br-1m6a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T20:52:40.649220290Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-1m6a.13","title":"Enhanced request log panel matching Python parity","description":"Enhance `render_http_request_panel()` to be theme-consistent and parity-aligned with the legacy Python request logging UX.

CURRENT STATE (ALREADY IMPLEMENTED)
- `LOG_JSON_ENABLED` exists in `mcp_agent_mail_core::Config` and is already honored.
  - When enabled: emit JSON line to stderr via `http_request_log_json_line()`.
  - When disabled: emit KV/structlog-like line.
- `render_http_request_panel()` exists and emits a 3-line ASCII panel.

WHAT'S MISSING
- Better method/status badge coloring (2xx/3xx/4xx/5xx + GET/POST/DELETE/etc).
- Theme unification (stop hardcoding ANSI colors inside this function).
- Respect rich-output gates so we don't dump ANSI art in non-TTY contexts.

TARGET STATE
1) Status + method styles (from theme `br-1m6a.12`)
- `fn status_style(code: u16) -> Style`
  - 2xx: SUCCESS, bold
  - 3xx: INFO
  - 4xx: WARNING, bold
  - 5xx: DANGER, bold
- `fn method_style(method: &str) -> Style`
  - GET: INFO
  - POST: PRIMARY
  - PUT/PATCH: ACCENT
  - DELETE: DANGER

2) Panel layout
- Title: `{METHOD}  {PATH}  {STATUS}  {MS}ms` with per-segment styles.
- Body: `client: {ip}` in muted style.
- Border: rounded + muted, width capped (keep deterministic, default 100).

3) Gating
- Only render the rich-ish panel when:
  - `config.log_rich_enabled == true`
  - `stdout.is_terminal() == true`
- Otherwise fall back to the existing plain-text fallback line.

TESTING
- Unit tests for style mapping:
  - status: 200, 301, 404, 500
  - method: GET, POST, DELETE
- Unit test that JSON stderr emission stays valid JSON.
- Unit tests that non-TTY or `LOG_RICH_ENABLED=false` results in fallback (no ANSI panel).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T20:52:40.753047760Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T23:48:14.170434696Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T23:48:14.170409869Z","close_reason":"Implemented: status_style, method_style, render_http_request_panel moved to console.rs with theme colors, rounded borders, log_rich_enabled gating. 13 new tests in console.rs + updated 6 tests in lib.rs. All 249 tests pass, 0 clippy warnings.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1m6a.13","depends_on_id":"br-1m6a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T20:52:40.753047760Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-1m6a.13","depends_on_id":"br-1m6a.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:15:43.613416111Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"br-1m6a.14","title":"Comprehensive unit tests for console output enhancements","description":"Ensure we have comprehensive, *stable* unit test coverage for all console-output enhancements (banner, tool panels, HUD, request panels, masking, theming, layout persistence).

IMPORTANT
- Console output is inherently brittle; these tests must focus on stable invariants and use robust ANSI/OSC normalization.
- Do NOT wait until the very end: this bead should progress incrementally as features land.

CURRENT STATE
- We already have meaningful unit tests in:
  - `crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/console.rs` (banner structure, tool panel structure, masking basics, sparkline sampling, toast formatting, log panel rendering)
  - `crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/lib.rs` (request panel + JSON emission helpers)
- But several of these tests encode behavior that we now intend to change (e.g. `project_key` masking, truncation defaults, config gating).

TEST HARNESS REQUIREMENTS (FOUNDATIONAL)
1) ANSI/OSC normalization helpers (shared across tests)
- Strip SGR (`\x1b[...m`), cursor movement, and OSC-8 hyperlinks.
- Provide `assert_contains_normalized(haystack, needle)` helpers.

2) Deterministic width handling
- Tests should render at fixed widths (80/100/120) and assert on headers/labels rather than exact spacing.

3) Theme determinism
- Any render test that depends on the active FrankenTUI theme must guard against global theme races using `ftui_extras::theme::ScopedThemeLock`.

COVERAGE CHECKLIST (WHAT MUST BE TESTED)
- Masking/sanitization (`br-1m6a.18`):
  - `project_key` + `storage_root` never masked
  - common secret keys are masked
  - URL credential stripping works
- Theme system (`br-1m6a.12`):
  - `CONSOLE_THEME` parsing and mapping
  - syntax highlighting uses `syntax_theme()` for coherence
- Banner (`br-1m6a.1`, `br-1m6a.9`, `br-1m6a.17`, `br-1m6a.10`):
  - required section headers exist
  - Web UI links include all expected paths
  - JSON stats blob parses as JSON after ANSI stripping
- Tool call panels (`br-1m6a.2`, `br-1m6a.6`, `br-1m6a.16`):
  - config gating matrix works (rich/tools/tool_calls toggles)
  - truncation uses config default + override
  - query stats section present when queries>0 and absent when 0
- Request panels (`br-1m6a.13`):
  - method/status style mapping and fallback gating
  - JSON log line validity
- HUD enhancements (`br-1m6a.3`, `br-1m6a.4`, `br-1m6a.7`, `br-1m6a.8`):
  - sparkline normalization math
  - toast queue push/expire
  - table theme effects do not panic
  - agent list renders/degrades with narrow widths
- Console layout config (`br-1m6a.19`):
  - percent/rows clamping
  - anchor parsing
  - persistence envfile writer idempotency
  - keybinding->state updates (pure helper; no PTY needed)
- Alt-screen left split mode (`br-1m6a.20`):
  - split ratio math/clamping
  - log buffer bounds
  - LogViewer state transitions (search/filter/follow) without sleeps
  - render smoke test (no panic)
- Command palette (`br-1m6a.21`):
  - action dispatch mapping updates config + persists when enabled
  - stable action ordering (deterministic)
- Structured event timeline (`br-1m6a.22`):
  - ring buffer overflow + stable ordering
  - filter/search logic
  - render smoke test (no panic)

STRATEGY
- Prefer a mix of:
  - Small pure-function unit tests (masking, normalization, parsing)
  - Render tests that assert on stable headers/keywords after ANSI stripping

DONE WHEN
- Each bead in `br-1m6a` has explicit unit tests for its core invariants.
- Tests are stable in CI (no flaky timing dependencies).","notes":"Progress: added integration-test ANSI/OSC stripping + normalization helpers under crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/tests/common/mod.rs, plus baseline console integration tests in crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/tests/console_output.rs (banner sections + param/result masking). Remaining: expand coverage to other console beads (request panels, HUD/timeline, palette) and centralize shared strip helpers for broader reuse.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"IndigoRidge","created_at":"2026-02-06T20:52:40.858032931Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T23:38:58.599119348Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1m6a.14","depends_on_id":"br-1m6a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T20:52:40.858032931Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"br-1m6a.15","title":"E2E smoke test for console output","description":"Add a dedicated E2E smoke test that validates the rich console output in a *real* TTY/PTY environment.

WHY
- Many console features are gated on `is_terminal()`; unit tests alone cannot prove real terminal behavior.
- We need stable, grep-able assertions with robust ANSI/OSC normalization.

LOCATION / HARNESS PATTERN
- Follow the repo’s existing E2E structure:
  - Implement core logic in `scripts/e2e_console.sh`.
  - Add wrapper `tests/e2e/test_console.sh` that calls the script (like `test_http.sh`, `test_stdio.sh`).
  - Reuse `scripts/e2e_lib.sh` helpers (tmp dirs, server lifecycle, assertions).

PTY CAPTURE
- Use `script -q` (or an equivalent PTY tool available in CI) to run the server so `stdout.is_terminal()==true`.
- Capture BOTH stdout and stderr to files for assertions.
- Add an ANSI/OSC stripper helper that normalizes:
  - SGR color codes (`\x1b[...m`)
  - OSC-8 hyperlinks (if emitted)
  - cursor movement / clear sequences (if any)

TEST CASES (MINIMUM)
1) `startup_banner_present_in_tty`
- Start server with:
  - `LOG_RICH_ENABLED=true`
  - `TOOLS_LOG_ENABLED=true`
- Assert banner contains:
  - logo/header marker
  - "Server Configuration"
  - "Database Statistics"
  - "Web UI"

2) `banner_suppressed_when_rich_disabled`
- Start server with `LOG_RICH_ENABLED=false` under PTY.
- Assert banner markers are absent.

3) `tool_call_panels_respect_gates`
- Start server with `LOG_RICH_ENABLED=true`, `TOOLS_LOG_ENABLED=true`, `LOG_TOOL_CALLS_ENABLED=true`.
- Perform one MCP tool call (use existing stdio/HTTP harness helpers) and assert:
  - TOOL CALL panel header appears
  - tool name appears
- Repeat with `LOG_TOOL_CALLS_ENABLED=false` and assert the TOOL CALL panel does NOT appear.

4) `request_panel_logged`
- Start HTTP server with `HTTP_REQUEST_LOG_ENABLED=true` and send a request to `/health/liveness`.
- Assert request panel (or fallback line) includes method/path/status.

5) `layout_config_reported`
- Start with layout env vars from `br-1m6a.19` and assert output contains the layout summary line.

6) `theme_config_reported`
- Start with `CONSOLE_THEME=high_contrast` and assert banner/config output mentions `high_contrast` (or the display name) so we know theme selection is wired.

7) `left_split_mode_engages`
- Start server with:
  - `CONSOLE_SPLIT_MODE=left`
  - `CONSOLE_SPLIT_RATIO_PERCENT=30`
- Assert the banner includes a plain-text layout summary like `Console: left 30% (alt-screen)`.
  - This is required so E2E does not need to parse alt-screen control codes.

STABILITY
- If PTY capture is unavailable, the test must skip with a clear message.
- Keep assertions resilient: assert on stable headers/keywords, not exact spacing/art.","notes":"PLAN-SPACE ADDITIONS (user request: TUI enabled by default + interactive layout remembers settings)

- Add E2E case: `default_rich_console_enabled`
  - Start HTTP server in a real PTY with `LOG_RICH_ENABLED` unset (not true/false) and assert banner markers are present.
  - This proves the default is rich when `stdout.is_terminal()==true`.

- Add E2E case: `persisted_console_settings_are_loaded`
  - Write a temp envfile containing only `CONSOLE_UI_HEIGHT_PERCENT=50` + `CONSOLE_UI_ANCHOR=top` + `CONSOLE_THEME=darcula`.
  - Start server with `CONSOLE_PERSIST_PATH` pointing at that file and without setting those vars in the real env.
  - Assert the banner includes the plain-text layout line reflecting the persisted settings (no ANSI parsing).

- Add (optional) E2E case: `interactive_change_persists`
  - Run server under PTY with `CONSOLE_INTERACTIVE=1`, `CONSOLE_AUTO_SAVE=1`, `CONSOLE_PERSIST_PATH=<temp>`.
  - Inject keypresses (e.g. `+` then `t`) and assert the envfile is updated with the new values.
  - Gate with `AM_E2E_INTERACTIVE=1` so CI can skip if PTY input is flaky.

- Logging: for every case, save *normalized* output (SGR + OSC stripped) as an artifact to make failures debuggable.

PROGRESS
- Implemented PTY console E2E suite:
  - `scripts/e2e_console.sh`
  - `tests/e2e/test_console.sh`
- Improved tempdir robustness: `scripts/e2e_lib.sh` now honors `TMPDIR` and defaults it to `/data/tmp` when available.
- Implemented interactive persistence harness (PTY key injection) gated by `AM_E2E_INTERACTIVE=1`.
- Verified locally:
  - `./scripts/e2e_test.sh console` passes (interactive skipped)
  - `AM_E2E_INTERACTIVE=1 ./scripts/e2e_test.sh console` passes (interactive enabled)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T20:52:40.964179560Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T23:51:21.701688188Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1m6a.15","depends_on_id":"br-1m6a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T20:52:40.964179560Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-1m6a.15","depends_on_id":"br-1m6a.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:46:38.866752426Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-1m6a.15","depends_on_id":"br-1m6a.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:46:39.075183315Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-1m6a.15","depends_on_id":"br-1m6a.14","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T20:52:49.771651393Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-1m6a.15","depends_on_id":"br-1m6a.16","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:46:39.281317798Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-1m6a.15","depends_on_id":"br-1m6a.19","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:46:39.181128829Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-1m6a.15","depends_on_id":"br-1m6a.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:46:38.970475633Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-1m6a.15","depends_on_id":"br-1m6a.20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:51:28.121284592Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

  - `LOG_RICH_ENABLED=true`
  - `TOOLS_LOG_ENABLED=true`

 {"id":"br-1m6a.18","title":"Sensitive value masking utility for console output","description":"Create a single, shared masking/sanitization utility so *all* console output (banner, tool-call panels, request panels, etc.) is safe by default.

WHY
- We must never render secrets into the terminal (bearer tokens, JWT secrets, API keys, passwords, etc.).
- Multiple console features need this (startup banner config table, tool-call args/result panels).
- Current `crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/console.rs` masking is a good start but is *not correct yet*:
  - It masks `project_key` (bad: it is a core identity signal we want visible).
  - It uses a non-ASCII bullet mask and includes overly-broad patterns like `auth`.

CORE API (SERVER CONSOLE MODULE)
- Keep these helpers in `crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/console.rs` (or a tiny sibling module imported by it).

1) `fn is_sensitive_key(key: &str) -> bool`
- Case-insensitive key matching.
- MUST have explicit allowlist exceptions first:
  - `project_key` MUST NOT be masked.
  - `storage_root` MUST NOT be masked.
- Sensitive substrings (case-insensitive):
  - `token`, `secret`, `password`, `credential`, `bearer`, `jwt`, `api_key`, `private_key`
- DO NOT treat `auth` as sensitive (too broad; would mask lots of safe boolean/enum fields).

2) `fn mask_sensitive_value(original: &str) -> String`
- Use an ASCII-only placeholder for maximum portability/determinism:
  - Recommended: `<redacted>`
- Keep it constant (do not depend on original length) to avoid width surprises.

3) `fn sanitize_known_value(key: &str, value: &str) -> Option<String>`
- Additional safety net for common non-obvious secret carriers where the *key* is not obviously sensitive:
  - `database_url`: if value looks like `scheme://user:pass@host/...`, mask only the password segment.
  - `*_redis_url` / `redis_url` / `http_rate_limit_redis_url`: same.
- Return `Some(sanitized)` if a transformation is applied; else `None`.
- Keep this conservative; prefer obvious, stable parsing over regex.

4) `fn mask_json(value: &serde_json::Value) -> serde_json::Value`
- Recursively walk objects/arrays.
- If an object key is sensitive: replace the value with the redaction placeholder.
- Else, recurse.
- Also apply `sanitize_known_value()` for known keys.

INTEGRATIONS (CALL SITES)
- Tool call start/end panels (`br-1m6a.2`): mask args *before* pretty-printing; mask result previews too.
- Startup banner config display (`br-1m6a.10`): sanitize DB/redis URLs; mask auth/bearer/JWT secrets.

TESTING (UNIT)
- `is_sensitive_key`:
  - Positive: bearer_token, api_key, password, HTTP_BEARER_TOKEN, jwt_secret, private_key
  - Negative: project_key, storage_root, endpoint, app_environment
  - Case-insensitive behavior
- `sanitize_known_value`:
  - postgres URL with credentials -> password redacted, host preserved
  - sqlite path -> unchanged
- `mask_json` recursion:
  - Nested objects + arrays
  - Empty objects
  - Non-string values (numbers/bools/null) under sensitive keys still redacted
  - `project_key` remains visible (regression)

PERF/SAFETY NOTES
- This runs only when we are about to emit console output.
- Avoid regex on the hot path; keep allocations reasonable.","notes":"Implementation note: is_sensitive_key also exact-matches authorization and auth_header as sensitive (common bearer-token carriers) while still avoiding broad auth substring masking. Tool-call end result previews are masked via JSON parse + mask_json before truncation.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"IndigoRidge","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:25:05.403325350Z","created_by":"CobaltTower","updated_at":"2026-02-06T23:27:39.967870031Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T23:27:39.967783289Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1m6a.18","depends_on_id":"br-1m6a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:25:05.403325350Z","created_by":"CobaltTower","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-1m6a.19","title":"Console layout configuration (HUD sizing + persistence)","description":"Make the StartupDashboard console layout user-configurable, interactively adjustable, and persistable.

BACKGROUND
- Today `StartupDashboard::maybe_start()` hardcodes inline HUD sizing:
  - `ui_height ~= term_height / 3`
  - `UiAnchor::Bottom`
- Users explicitly want to control how much screen is "static HUD" vs "scrolling logs":
  - bottom 20%, bottom 50%
  - left 30% (requires AltScreen + internal log viewer)

GOALS
- Rich HUD remains enabled by default in real TTYs (`LOG_RICH_ENABLED=true` default).
- Config can be set via env vars and persisted to a user-local config envfile.
- Interactive configuration is available by default for humans in HTTP mode (STDIO mode cannot steal stdin from MCP protocol).
- Persisted settings are automatically remembered (no manual edits required).

SCOPE (PHASED)

PHASE 1 (P0): ENV-DRIVEN LAYOUT CONFIG (NO INTERACTIVE INPUT)
Add config fields to `mcp_agent_mail_core::Config` + `from_env()` parsing.

Inline HUD sizing:
- `console_ui_height_percent: u16` (default 33, clamp 10..80)
- `console_ui_anchor: ConsoleUiAnchor` (default bottom; values: bottom|top)
- `console_ui_auto_size: bool` (default false)
- `console_inline_auto_min_rows: u16` (default 8, clamp >= 4)
- `console_inline_auto_max_rows: u16` (default 18, clamp >= min_rows)

Split mode config (required for "left 30%"):
- `console_split_mode: ConsoleSplitMode` (default `inline`; values: inline|left)
- `console_split_ratio_percent: u16` (default 30, clamp 10..80)

Console theme selection (FrankenTUI)
- `CONSOLE_THEME` is defined in `br-1m6a.12` and must be persisted in the same user envfile so humans can tune the look-and-feel once and have it stick.
  - Values: `cyberpunk_aurora|darcula|lumen_light|nordic_frost|high_contrast`
  - Default: `cyberpunk_aurora`

Env vars:
- `CONSOLE_UI_HEIGHT_PERCENT`
- `CONSOLE_UI_ANCHOR`
- `CONSOLE_UI_AUTO_SIZE`
- `CONSOLE_INLINE_AUTO_MIN_ROWS`
- `CONSOLE_INLINE_AUTO_MAX_ROWS`
- `CONSOLE_SPLIT_MODE`
- `CONSOLE_SPLIT_RATIO_PERCENT`
- `CONSOLE_THEME`

Server integration (`crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/lib.rs`):
- If `console_split_mode=inline`:
  - If `console_ui_auto_size=false`: `ScreenMode::Inline { ui_height }`
  - Else: `ScreenMode::InlineAuto { min_height, max_height }`
  - Anchor uses `UiAnchor::{Bottom,Top}`
- If `console_split_mode=left`:
  - Use `ScreenMode::AltScreen` and delegate rendering to `br-1m6a.20` (log viewer + left HUD).

Banner/HUD discoverability:
- Add a single plain-text line describing the active console layout config:
  - e.g. `Console: inline bottom 33%` / `Console: inline_auto top 8..18 rows` / `Console: left 30% (alt-screen)`
  - E2E must assert on this line (no parsing of control sequences).

PHASE 2 (P1): INTERACTIVE CONFIG (HTTP MODE ONLY)
This must be available by default for humans (TTY + HTTP mode), and safe in CI.

GATING
- Only enable interactive config when:
  - HTTP server mode
  - `stdin.is_terminal()==true`
  - `config.log_rich_enabled==true`
- Provide an env override:
  - `CONSOLE_INTERACTIVE=0` disables
  - `CONSOLE_INTERACTIVE=1` forces enable (even if we later default it on)

KEYBINDINGS (MINIMUM)
- `+` / `Up`: increase `console_ui_height_percent` by 5
- `-` / `Down`: decrease by 5
- `t` / `b`: toggle anchor top/bottom
- `a`: toggle inline auto-size
- `[` / `]`: decrease/increase `console_split_ratio_percent` by 5 (when in left split)
- `i`: switch to inline mode
- `l`: switch to left split mode
- `?`: toggle a help overlay (shows current values + keys)

APPLYING CHANGES
- Inline height/anchor changes should apply live.
- Switching `console_split_mode` may require recreating the `TerminalWriter` (drop + re-init). If live switching is too risky initially:
  - persist the new mode and show a toast/log line "Restart required to apply console mode change".

PERSISTENCE (AUTO-REMEMBER SETTINGS)
- Persist to a user-local envfile (NOT repo `.env`):
  - default: `~/.config/mcp-agent-mail/config.env`
  - override: `CONSOLE_PERSIST_PATH=/path/to/file`
- Read this envfile at startup so console settings apply automatically:
  - For `CONSOLE_*` keys: precedence `real env > persisted user envfile > defaults`
  - Do NOT fall back to working-directory `.env` for `CONSOLE_*` keys (avoid repo contamination)
- File format: `KEY=value` lines (envfile style) so the existing config loader can ingest it.
- Auto-save by default:
  - `CONSOLE_AUTO_SAVE` (default true)
  - Save ONLY the console-related keys (including `CONSOLE_THEME`).
  - Preserve unrelated lines/comments when updating an existing file.

TESTING
- Unit tests (core config): parsing, clamping, defaults.
- Unit tests (server): percent->rows mapping for small terminals; anchor mapping.
- Unit tests (persistence): envfile update is idempotent and preserves unrelated content.
- Unit tests (interactive): factor key->state updates into a pure helper so we can test each keybinding without needing raw-mode PTY input.
- E2E (`br-1m6a.15`): start server with layout env vars and assert banner reports active layout.

NOTES
- STDIO mode must never start an input reader thread.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CobaltTower","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:25:32.023878914Z","created_by":"CobaltTower","updated_at":"2026-02-06T23:48:46.020572657Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T23:48:46.020545025Z","close_reason":"Completed: CONSOLE_* layout config + persistence + interactive keybindings + unit tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1m6a.19","depends_on_id":"br-1m6a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:25:32.023878914Z","created_by":"CobaltTower","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-1m6a.2","title":"Tool call start/end logging panels with syntax highlighting","description":"Upgrade per-tool-call console logging to be FrankenTUI-native and parity-aligned with the legacy Python `rich_logger.py` tool panels.

-{"id":"br-1m6a.20","title":"Alt-screen left split console: HUD + LogViewer log pane","description":"Implement the "left 30%" style console layout: a two-pane, AltScreen dashboard where the HUD is static on the left and a scrollable/searchable log viewer lives on the right.

WHY
- Inline mode preserves terminal scrollback, but it cannot do a true left/right HUD vs log split.
- Users explicitly want layouts like "left 30%" and want to configure this interactively.

CONFIG / INPUTS
- Uses layout config from `br-1m6a.19`:
  - `CONSOLE_SPLIT_MODE=left`
  - `CONSOLE_SPLIT_RATIO_PERCENT` (e.g., 30)

GATING

  - `config.log_rich_enabled == true`
  - `stdout.is_terminal() == true`
  - running in HTTP server mode (stdio mode cannot safely run an interactive UI loop)

CORE UI (ALTSCREEN)
- Use `ftui::ScreenMode::AltScreen` for the dashboard writer.
- Render a 2-column layout:
  1) Left pane: existing HUD (reuse `render_dashboard_frame` or a factored-out HUD widget)
  2) Right pane: a real log viewer widget (see below)

LOG VIEWER (USE FRANKENTUI WIDGET)
- Do NOT hand-roll a bespoke scroll/search UI.
- Use `ftui_widgets::log_viewer::{LogViewer, LogViewerState}`.
  - Demo reference: `/dp/frankentui/crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/log_search.rs`
- Behavior:
  - Follow mode on by default (tail -f semantics)
  - `/` opens search bar
  - `n` / `N`: next/prev match
  - `f`: filter toggle (show only matching lines)
  - Up/Down/PageUp/PageDown: scroll
  - End: jump to latest
  - `?`: help overlay

  - Demo reference: `/dp/frankentui/crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/notifications.rs`

GOALS
- Toasts are enabled by default when rich console is active in a real TTY.
- Non-HUD / non-TTY contexts must stay grep-friendly: degrade to a plain log line (no ANSI art dumps).
- Keep behavior testable without sleeps.

IMPLEMENTATION
1) Use FrankenTUI queue types
- Add to `StartupDashboard`:
  - `notifications: Mutex<ftui_widgets::notification_queue::NotificationQueue>`
- Queue config (initial):
  - `max_visible`: 3-4
  - `max_queued`: 20
  - position: `ToastPosition::TopRight` (or inside HUD area if we restrict drawing to the HUD rect)

2) API surface
- `fn push_toast(&self, toast: Toast, prio: NotificationPriority)`
- Convenience helpers:
  - `toast_info(msg)`, `toast_warning(msg)`, `toast_error(msg)`

3) Rendering
- In the dashboard render path, render `NotificationStack::new(&queue)` as an overlay.
  - If overlaying over the whole terminal is not safe in Inline mode, render it inside the HUD area only.

4) Non-HUD degradation
- If no dashboard is active (stdio mode or non-TTY), toast events become plain one-line logs.
  - Prefer `console_info/console_warning/console_error` once `br-1m6a.5` exists.
  - Otherwise: `tracing::info!/warn!/error!` or `ftui_runtime::ftui_println!()` with plain text.

EVENT HOOKS (INITIAL SET)
- In tool dispatch/instrumentation paths, trigger toasts for:
  - `register_agent` success: "Agent {name} registered ({program})"
  - `file_reservation_paths`: conflict -> warning toast with conflict summary
  - `force_release_file_reservation` success -> warning toast
  - repeated server errors: if >5 errors in 10s -> error toast
  - messaging bursts: if >20 send/reply in 5s -> info toast

ACTIONS (OPTIONAL, NICE-TO-HAVE)
- For some toasts, include actions:
  - "Copy endpoint" (copies URL to clipboard if available; otherwise just logs)
  - "Open web UI" (only if we ever support shell-out; otherwise omit)

TESTING
- Unit tests for queue behavior (no sleeps):
  - push respects max_queued
  - urgent priority jumps ahead
  - dismiss/dismiss_all behavior
  - auto-expiry can be simulated by mutating `toast.state.created_at` to an old instant, then calling `queue.tick(...)` (no wall-clock sleeps)
- Render smoke test: overlay render does not panic for empty/1/4 visible toasts.

NOTE
- If time-based animations make tests flaky, disable animations for our toasts (use reduced motion config) and keep assertions on structure/content only.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T20:52:00.360557085Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T23:39:30.256372808Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T23:39:30.252273375Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1m6a.4","depends_on_id":"br-1m6a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T20:52:00.360557085Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"br-1r9v","title":"Console layout config: HUD sizing + persistence","description":"Add interactive console layout configuration allowing users to control HUD size/position vs scrolling log output, with automatic persistence.

USER NEED:
- Configure how much screen real estate the static HUD occupies vs the scrolling log.
  - Examples: bottom 20%, bottom 50%.
- Do this interactively and have settings remembered.

DEFAULTS (must be great out-of-the-box):
- Rich HUD starts automatically in a real TTY when `LOG_RICH_ENABLED=true` (default).
- Default HUD size is ~33% of terminal height, anchored bottom.

CONFIGURABLE SETTINGS (env vars + persisted user config):
1) `CONSOLE_UI_HEIGHT_PERCENT`: u8 (10-80, default 33)
   - Maps to: `ui_height = (term_height * pct / 100).max(8).min(term_height - 2)`

2) `CONSOLE_UI_ANCHOR`: enum {bottom, top} (default bottom)
   - Maps to `ftui::UiAnchor`

3) `CONSOLE_UI_AUTO_SIZE`: bool (default false)
   - When true: use `ScreenMode::InlineAuto { min_height, max_height }` instead of fixed.

4) `CONSOLE_AUTO_SAVE`: bool (default true)
   - When true, interactive adjustments persist immediately.

PERSISTENCE FILE:
- `~/.config/agent-mail/console.toml`
  - Priority: env vars > console.toml > defaults
  - Saving should preserve stable formatting (don’t churn on every save).

INTERACTIVE ADJUSTMENT:
- HTTP server mode only (stdio mode uses stdin for MCP protocol).
- Spawn a lightweight `/dev/tty` (or stdin) reader thread when running in a real TTY:
  - Up / '+': increase `ui_height_percent` by 5 (max 80)
  - Down / '-': decrease `ui_height_percent` by 5 (min 10)
  - 't'/'b': toggle anchor top/bottom
  - 'a': toggle auto-size
  - 's': force-save settings
  - 'q': disable interactive adjustments for this run
- HUD should display a subtle, dim hint line when interactive mode is active:
  - `+/- resize | t/b anchor | a autosize | s save`

INTEGRATION NOTES:
- Left/right splits ("left 30%") require AltScreen + an internal log viewer.
  - That’s implemented separately in `br-1m6a.19`.

IMPLEMENTATION:
- Add config fields to `mcp_agent_mail_core::Config` + `from_env()` parsing.
- Add load/save for `console.toml` (no new global config system; just this file).
- Modify `StartupDashboard::maybe_start()` to use configured values instead of hardcoded 1/3.

TESTING:
- Config loading priority (env > file > defaults)
- `ui_height` calculation + clamp behavior
- Anchor parsing fallback
- Save/load roundtrip for console.toml
- Interactive key handling logic (unit test the state machine; no real tty required)

ACCEPTANCE:
- Users can resize HUD interactively in HTTP mode and it persists.
- Defaults remain enabled and sane with zero config.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:18:52.502187876Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T21:38:30.839103222Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T21:38:30.839080589Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of br-1m6a.19 (merged console layout plan there).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
